events.title: US Vows 'Very Hard' Strikes on Iran and Seizure of Oil Terminals
events.desc: US President Donald Trump vowed fresh attacks against Iran, stating American forces would also soon seize the country's key oil infrastructure. This threat comes after Iran warned that the fragile ceasefire in their three-month war was 'practically meaningless' following recent US strikes, to which Tehran responded with attacks around the region.

events.title: US and Iran Exchange Strikes Across Middle East for Second Day
events.desc: The United States and Iran exchanged strikes across the Middle East for a second consecutive day. Iran stated it responded to US strikes by targeting American assets in Kuwait, Jordan, and Bahrain, with Bahrain reporting damage to its capital city overnight.

events.title: Three Indian Sailors Killed in US Strike on Oil Tanker
events.desc: Three Indian sailors died in a US military strike on a tanker off Oman, as part of Washington's efforts to blockade Iran-linked shipping. India has summoned a US diplomat to demand an end to such attacks, noting these are the first reported deaths since the blockade began.

events.title: US Strikes Iran in Response to Downing of Military Helicopter
events.desc: President Donald Trump accused Iran of shooting down a US military helicopter over the Strait of Hormuz and vowed a response. Subsequently, US forces launched strikes against Iran.

events.title: Israeli Strikes in Southern Lebanon Kill 17
events.desc: Israeli air strikes in southern Lebanon resulted in the deaths of at least 17 people. Nine of the casualties were reported in a series of attacks in the town of Tayr Debba, according to Lebanon's state news agency.

events.title: Iran and Israel Agree to Pause Strikes, Warn of Retaliation
events.desc: Israel and Iran have agreed to a temporary pause in their strikes, with both sides warning of retaliation if the ceasefire is breached again. Israel's Prime Minister stated the country is holding fire 'at the moment,' following Iran's armed forces announcing they had stopped military action.

events.title: Israel Strikes Beirut Suburb Days After US-Brokered Truce
events.desc: Israel launched strikes on a Beirut suburb, days after a US-brokered truce. Israel stated the attacks were ordered "in response to Hezbollah's firing at Israeli territory."

events.title: One Dead, Five Injured in Shooting Incident in Israel
events.desc: A shooting incident in Israel resulted in one death and five injuries. The suspect reportedly opened fire from a car in multiple locations before being shot dead by security forces.

events.title: Funeral Held for Baby Shot Dead by Israeli Troops in West Bank
events.desc: A funeral was held for a seven-month-old baby who was shot and killed by Israeli troops in the occupied West Bank. The baby's body was carried by his father, wrapped in a Palestinian flag.

events.title: At least 22 killed as Pakistani military helicopter crashes in Kashmir
events.desc: An inquiry has been launched after a Pakistani military helicopter crashed in Pakistan-administered Kashmir, killing at least 22 people. Officials cited a 'technical fault' as the cause of the Mi-17 crash.

events.title: Video Shows Aftermath of Iranian Attack in Bahrain
events.desc: Video footage shows damage to cars, homes, and buildings in Hamad Town and Manama following Iranian strikes in Bahrain.

events.title: India Demands End to US Attacks on Ships After Three Sailors Killed
events.desc: India has demanded an end to US attacks on ships after three Indian sailors died in a US military strike on a tanker off Oman. The incident occurred as part of Washington's efforts to blockade Iran-linked shipping.

events.title: Israel to Allocate Funds for West Bank Settlement Expansion
events.desc: Israel is reportedly set to approve the allocation of 1 billion shekels ($337.8 million) to build new settlements and connect them to infrastructure in the occupied West Bank. The plan is being promoted by the far-right finance minister Bezalel Smotrich.

events.title: UK and Allies Sanction Networks Enabling Settler Violence in West Bank
events.desc: The UK and its allies have imposed sanctions on networks facilitating settler violence in the West Bank. France will also bar far-right Israeli Minister Bezalel Smotrich from entry as part of these measures, which Israel has condemned.

events.title: AirAsia X Postpones Bahrain Flight Route Due to Regional Conflict
events.desc: Malaysian airline AirAsia X has postponed the launch of its planned service via Bahrain until at least August due to the ongoing conflict in the Middle East. The route was initially scheduled to commence in June.

events.title: East African Ministers to Unveil Budgets Amid Iran Cost Shocks and Debt Strains
events.desc: Finance ministers in Kenya, Uganda, and Tanzania are set to present their 2026/27 budgets amid concerns about economic shocks linked to the Middle East war and existing debt strains. Investors are focused on how these economies will be shielded from cost fluctuations.

events.title: Iran Shuts Strait of Hormuz
events.desc: Iran has announced the closure of the Strait of Hormuz. This crucial maritime route is responsible for shipping approximately 20 percent of global oil and natural gas in peacetime.

events.title: Pentagon's AI Edge Threatened by Publicly Released Frontier Models
events.desc: Adversaries can undermine the Pentagon's AI advantage by harvesting the logic of publicly released frontier AI models. This poses a significant risk as the Department of Defense increasingly relies on AI-first warfighting systems, where military predominance is tied to AI model supremacy.

events.title: Satellite Imagery Shows Ongoing Demolitions Across Southern Lebanon
events.desc: Despite a fragile ceasefire, satellite imagery reveals extensive demolitions in southern Lebanon. At least 46 out of 54 towns and villages within the Israel Defense Forces' (IDF) 'Yellow Line' have been heavily damaged or flattened.

events.title: Banned Russian Submunitions Found After Mali's Military Announces Airstrikes
events.desc: Unexploded Russian-made cluster munition bomblets have been discovered in northern Mali, along with damage consistent with their impact. This finding comes despite Mali being a state party to the Convention on Cluster Munitions.

events.title: US-Sponsored Talks Between Israel and Lebanon Continue Amidst Escalation
events.desc: US-sponsored talks between Israel and the Lebanese government are ongoing in an effort to negotiate with Israel and disarm Hezbollah. However, intensified fighting has continued, with Hezbollah rejecting a proposed ceasefire deal.

events.title: Investigation Exposes Alleged Israeli Torture and Sexual Violence Against Palestinian Detainees
events.desc: An investigation reveals decades of alleged Israeli torture and sexual violence against Palestinian detainees. The report details numerous alleged abuses.

events.title: World Chess Body Suspends Russia Over Activities in Occupied Ukraine
events.desc: The World Chess Federation has suspended Russia, accusing it of usurting control of the game in areas of Ukraine captured by Russian forces since 2022. This action highlights the international repercussions of the ongoing conflict.

events.title: Palestine Action Activists Face Potential UK 'Terror' Sentences
events.desc: Four members of the Palestine Action group could face 'terror' sentences in the UK over their involvement in an Elbit factory raid in 2024. This case raises questions about the definition and application of terror charges.

events.title: Pope Francis Makes Appeal for Migrants in Canary Islands
events.desc: Pope Francis will meet with approximately 1,000 migrants in Spain's Canary Islands who have undertaken dangerous journeys across the Atlantic to reach Europe. The Pope's visit underscores the ongoing migrant crisis.

events.title: Kushner's Albania Resort Criticized as Environmental Disaster
events.desc: An on-site observation reveals that Kushner's resort project in Albania has caused significant environmental damage at the Vjosa-Narta wetlands, with bulldozers reportedly tearing into the sensitive ecosystem.

events.title: Russian Shadow Fleet Operations Highlight Sanctions Evasion Tactics
events.desc: Russia's 'shadow fleet' continues to operate, with tankers catching fire or being added to sanctions lists. The larger issue is the ships that evade detection, moving between registries and shell companies, a scheme refined at scale from North Korea's previous operations.

events.title: Demolitions and Damage in Southern Lebanon Detailed via Satellite Imagery
events.desc: Ongoing demolitions and significant damage have been observed across southern Lebanon, with satellite imagery showing that a large majority of towns and villages within a designated zone have been heavily impacted or flattened, despite a recent ceasefire.
